passages.title: The Development of Plastics
passages.source_html:
The Development of Plastics 


A 


When rubber was first commercially produced in Europe during the nineteenth century, it rapidly became a very important commodity, particularly in the fields of transportation and electricity. However, during the twentieth century a number of new synthetic materials, called plastics, superseded natural rubber in all but a few applications. 


B 


Rubber is a polymer – a compound containing large molecules that are formed by the bonding of many smaller, simpler units, repeated over and over again. The same bonding principle – polymerisation – underlies the creation of a huge range of plastics by the chemical industry. 


C 


The first plastic was developed as a result of a competition in the USA. In the 1860s, $10,000 was offered to anybody who could replace ivory – supplies of which were declining – with something equally good as a material for making billiard balls. The prize was won by John Wesley Hyatt with a material called celluloid. Celluloid was made by dissolving cellulose, a carbohydrate derived from plants, in a solution of camphor dissolved in ethanol. This new material rapidly found uses in the manufacture of products such as knife handles, detachable collars and cuffs, spectacle frames and photographic film. Without celluloid, the film industry could never have got off the ground at the end of the 19th century. 


D 


Celluloid can be repeatedly softened and reshaped by heat and is known as a thermoplastic. In 1907 Leo Baekeland, a Belgian chemist working in the USA invented a different kind of plastic by causing phenol and formaldehyde to react together. Baekeland called the material Bakelite, and it was the first of the thermosets – plastics that can be cast and moulded while hot but cannot be softened by heat and reshaped once they have set. Bakelite was a good insulator and was resistant to water, acids and moderate heat. With these properties, it was soon being used in the manufacture of switches, household items, such as knife handles, and electrical components for cars. 


E 


Soon chemists began looking for other small molecules that could be strung together to make polymers. In the 1930s, British chemists discovered that the gas ethylene would polymerise under heat and pressure to form a thermoplastic they called polythene. Polypropylene followed in the 1950s. both were used to make bottles, pipes and plastic bags. A small change in the starting material – replacing a hydrogen atom in ethylene with a chlorine atom – produced PVC (polyvinyl chloride), a hard, fireproof plastic suitable for drains and gutters. And by adding certain chemicals, a soft form of PVC could be produced, suitable as a substitute for rubber in items such as waterproof clothing. A closely related plastic was Teflon, as PTFE (polytetrafluoroethylene). This had a very low coefficient of friction, making it ideal for bearings, rollers, and non-stick frying pans. Polystyrene, developed during the 1930s in Germany, was a clear, glass-like material, used in food containers, domestic appliances and toys. Expanded polystyrene – a white, rigid foam – was widely used in packaging and insulation. Polyurethanes, also developed in Germany, found uses as adhesives, coatings, and – in the form of rigid foams – as insulation materials. They are all produced from chemicals derived from crude oil, which contains exactly the same elements – carbon and hydrogen – as many plastics. 


F 


The first of the man-made fibres, nylon, was also created in the 1930s. Its inventor was a chemist called Wallace Carothers, who worked for the Du Pont company in the USA. He found that under the right conditions, two chemicals – hexamethylenediamine and adipic acid — would form a polymer that could be pumped out through holes and then stretched to form long glossy threads that could be woven like silk. Its first use was to make parachutes for the US armed forces in World War II. In the post-war years, nylon completely replaced silk in the manufacture of stockings. Subsequently, many other synthetic fibres joined nylon, including Orion, Acrilan and Terylene. Today most garments are made of a blend of natural fibres, such as cotton and wool, and man-made fibres that make fabrics easier to look after. 


G 


The great strength of the plastic is its indestructibility. However, this quality is also something of a drawback: beaches all over the world, even on the remotest islands, are littered with plastic bottles that nothing can destroy. Nor is it very easy to recycle plastics, as different types of plastic are often used in the same items and call for different treatments. Plastics can be made biodegradable by incorporating into their structure a material such as starch, which is attacked by bacteria and causes the plastic to fall apart. Other materials can be incorporated that gradually decay in sunlight – although bottles made of such materials have to be stored in the dark, to ensure that they do not disintegrate before they have been used.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14-20 


Choose NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 14-20 on your answer sheet.


14

14

正确答案： 14 Photographic film##photographic film
Question 14

原文答案句：Without celluloid, the film industry could never have got off the ground at the end of the 19th century.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是图表中某个塑料制品的具体用途。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的图表信息，可以定位到C段中提到的"photographic film"。

解题思路：C段中提到"Celluloid was made by dissolving cellulose, a carbohydrate derived from plants, in a solution of camphor dissolved in ethanol. This new material rapidly found uses in the manufacture of products such as knife handles, detachable collars and cuffs, spectacle frames and photographic film."这句话明确指出"photographic film"是其中一个应用。因此，答案是"Photographic film"。


15

15 

正确答案： 15 Bakelite##bakelite
Question 15

原文答案句：In 1907 Leo Baekeland, a Belgian chemist working in the USA invented a different kind of plastic by causing phenol and formaldehyde to react together. Baekeland called the material Bakelite, and it was the first of the thermosets – plastics that can be cast and moulded while hot but cannot be softened by heat and reshaped once they have set.  

对应段落：D段  

审题：题目问的是哪种塑料是由酚和甲醛反应制成的。  

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"phenol and formaldehyde"定位到D段中"causing phenol and formaldehyde to react together"这一句子。  

解题思路：原文中明确提到，Leo Baekeland通过使酚和甲醛反应制造出一种名为Bakelite的塑料，并且这是第一种热固性塑料。因此，答案为Bakelite。


16

16 

正确答案： 16 electric switches##switches
Question 16

原文答案句：Bakelite was a good insulator and was resistant to water, acids and moderate heat. With these properties, it was soon being used in the manufacture of switches, household items, such as knife handles, and electrical components for cars.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是贝克勒尔发明的塑料在制造哪些物品时被使用。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"electric switches"或"switches"可以定位到D段中提到贝克勒尔发明的塑料被用于制造开关。

解题思路：根据原文中D段的句子可以确定，贝克勒尔发明的塑料很快被用于制造开关、家用物品（如刀柄）和汽车电气元件。因此，题目中的"electric switches"或"switches"就是答案。


17

17 

正确答案： 17 Britain##UK
Question 17

原文答案句：British chemists discovered that the gas ethylene would polymerise under heat and pressure to form a thermoplastic they called polythene.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求找出发现polythene的国家。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"polythene"定位到E段中提到这种塑料的句子。

解题思路：原文中明确提到"British chemists"（英国化学家）发现了polythene，因此答案为Britain或UK。


18

18 

正确答案： 18 fireproof
Question 18

原文答案句：A hard, fireproof plastic suitable for drains and gutters.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求从文章中选出不超过三个单词作为答案，填入指定的答题框中。根据题目信息，需要找出一种塑料的特性。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“fireproof”来定位答案在文章中的位置。在阅读文章时，注意寻找与“fireproof”相关的句子。

解题思路：在E段中，提到了一种名为PVC（polyvinyl chloride）的塑料，描述为“a hard, fireproof plastic suitable for drains and gutters.”根据题目要求，我们需要选择不超过三个单词作为答案，因此可以选择“fireproof”作为答案。这个词描述了PVC塑料的特性，即防火。因此，答案为“fireproof”。


19

19 

正确答案： 19 glass
Question 19

原文答案句：Polystyrene, developed during the 1930s in Germany, was a clear, glass-like material, used in food containers, domestic appliances and toys.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求从文章中选出不超过三个单词的答案，描述聚苯乙烯（Polystyrene）的某种特性。

定位关键信息：根据题目中可能出现的“Polystyrene”或“聚苯乙烯”等关键词，在文章中定位到E段描述聚苯乙烯的句子。

解题思路：原文中明确提到聚苯乙烯是一种“clear, glass-like material”（清晰、类似玻璃的材料），其中“glass-like”直接描述了其类似玻璃的特性。题目要求填写描述其特性的单词，因此“glass”是答案。


20

20 

正确答案： 20 rigid foams
Question 20

原文答案句：Expanded polystyrene – a white, rigid foam – was widely used in packaging and insulation.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求填写一种塑料的具体形式或用途，根据上下文推断与包装和绝缘相关的塑料形式。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“packaging and insulation”定位到E段末句，该句描述了expanded polystyrene的用途。

解题思路：原文中明确提到expanded polystyrene是一种“white, rigid foam”，并且被广泛用于“packaging and insulation”。题目问的是这种塑料的具体形式，因此答案为“rigid foams”。


Questions 21-26 

Do the following statements agree with the information given in the reading passage? In boxes 21-26 on your answer sheet, write TRUE if the statement is true FALSE if the statement is false NOT GIVEN if the information is not given in the passage

21

The chemical structure of plastic is very different from that of rubber.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Rubber is a polymer – a compound containing large molecules that are formed by the bonding of many smaller, simpler units, repeated over and over again. The same bonding principle – polymerisation – underlies the creation of a huge range of plastics by the chemical industry.  

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目问“塑料的化学结构与橡胶非常不同”，需要判断这一陈述是否与文章信息一致。  

定位关键信息：利用题目中的“chemical structure of plastic”和“rubber”定位，找到B段对比橡胶和塑料的化学结构。  

解题思路：原文明确指出橡胶是一种聚合物（polymer），其分子由许多小单位通过聚合反应（polymerisation）形成；塑料的创造也基于相同的聚合原理。这说明两者的化学结构原理相同，并非“非常不同”。因此，题目陈述与原文信息矛盾，答案为B（False）。


22

John Wesley was a famous chemist.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

原文答案句：The prize was won by John Wesley Hyatt with a material called celluloid.

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问"John Wesley是一位著名的化学家"，要求判断这个陈述是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的"John Wesley"定位到C段第一句，提到John Wesley Hyatt赢得了竞赛，发明了赛璐珞。

解题思路：原文提到John Wesley Hyatt赢得了发明赛璐珞的竞赛，但并未提及他是否是著名的化学家。文章只描述了他的发明成就，没有说明他的职业身份或知名度。因此，题目中的"著名化学家"这一信息在文章中没有明确给出，无法判断其真假。


23

Celluloid and Bakelite react to heat in the same way.

正确答案： B. FALSE

原文答案句：Celluloid can be repeatedly softened and reshaped by heat and is known as a thermoplastic. In 1907 Leo Baekeland, a Belgian chemist working in the USA invented a different kind of plastic by causing phenol and formaldehyde to react together. Baekeland called the material Bakelite, and it was the first of the thermosets – plastics that can be cast and moulded while hot but cannot be softened by heat and reshaped once they have set.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是Celluloid和Bakelite对热反应的方式是否相同。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的"Celluloid and Bakelite"和"react to heat"定位到D段，该段描述了这两种材料对热的反应方式。

解题思路：D段明确指出Celluloid是一种热塑性塑料，可以被反复加热软化并重塑；而Bakelite是一种热固性塑料，一旦成型就不能被加热软化或重塑。因此，这两种材料对热的反应方式不同，题目中的陈述与文章信息不符，所以答案是False。


24

The mix of different varieties of plastic can make them less recyclable.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：Nor is it very easy to recycle plastics, as different types of plastic are often used in the same items and call for different treatments.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问“不同种类塑料的混合会使它们更难回收”这一陈述是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词"mix of different varieties of plastic"和"less recyclable"定位到G段末句，该句提到不同类型的塑料需要不同的处理方法，暗示混合后回收困难。

解题思路：原文明确指出不同类型的塑料在同一物品中需要不同处理，这直接支持了“混合使回收更困难”的观点。因此，题目陈述与原文信息一致，答案为TRUE。


25

Adding starch into plastic does not necessarily make plastic more durable.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：Plastics can be made biodegradable by incorporating into their structure a material such as starch, which is attacked by bacteria and causes the plastic to fall apart.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是“将淀粉添加到塑料中并不一定会使塑料更耐用”这一说法是否与文章信息一致。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"starch"和"plastic"定位到G段，该段提到了将淀粉添加到塑料中的作用。

解题思路：原文中提到，将淀粉添加到塑料中可以使塑料变得可生物降解，即被细菌分解而分解。题目中的"more durable"意为更耐用，而原文中提到的是使塑料更容易被分解，这与"more durable"的含义相反。因此，题目中的说法与原文信息一致，答案为True。


26

Some plastic containers have to be preserved in special conditions.

正确答案： A. TRUE

原文答案句：bottles made of such materials have to be stored in the dark, to ensure that they do not disintegrate before they have been used.

对应段落：G段

审题：题目问的是“一些塑料容器需要保存在特殊条件下”，即询问塑料容器是否有需要特殊保存的情况。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词“plastic containers”和“preserved in special conditions”可以定位到G段，该段提到了塑料容器需要保存在黑暗中，以防止它们在使用前分解。

解题思路：根据原文答案句“bottles made of such materials have to be stored in the dark, to ensure that they do not disintegrate before they have been used.”，可以确定确实存在一些塑料容器需要保存在特殊条件下（即黑暗中），因此题目的陈述与原文信息一致，答案为TRUE。
